attention_maintenance.title: Attention maintenance
attention_maintenance.description: Difficulty sustaining focus without mental drift or cognitive fatigue over extended periods.

cognitive_load.title: Cognitive load
cognitive_load.description: Mental overload from simultaneously managing multiple priorities or information streams.

decision_fatigue.title: Decision fatigue
decision_fatigue.description: Degraded decision quality or executive function from repeated or high-stakes choices.

task_switching.title: Task switching
task_switching.description: Difficulty shifting between contexts with interference carryover or restart friction.

claims.claim_statement: L-theanine may support sustained attention and reduce mind-wandering without producing sedation.
claims.evidence_summary: Small human EEG and attention studies associate L-theanine with increased alpha-wave activity and improved selective attention under mild cognitive demand.
claims.limitations: Evidence base is small, tasks are often artificial, and effects may be subtle without caffeine co-administration.
claims.best_fit: Quiet sustained-attention tasks where sedation is not acceptable.
claims.not_best_fit: High-stimulation environments, panic-prone profiles, or expectations of strong stimulant-like focus.

claims.claim_statement: L-theanine combined with caffeine has the strongest acute evidence for reducing task-switching errors and attention carryover.
claims.evidence_summary: Human trials combining L-theanine and caffeine show improved cognitive flexibility, reaction time, and reduced commission errors during multi-task paradigms.
claims.limitations: Benefits are largely attributed to the combination, not theanine alone; dose matching matters.
claims.best_fit: Context-switching work environments where caffeine is already in use and cognitive smoothing is the goal.
claims.not_best_fit: Caffeine-sensitive users, anxious profiles, or situations where a pure sedation-free profile is needed without caffeine.

claims.claim_statement: Bacopa monnieri has the most consistent human trial evidence among botanicals for sustained attention and information processing speed.
claims.evidence_summary: Multiple RCTs in healthy adults report improvements in attention, processing speed, and visual information retention after 8-12 weeks of supplementation.
claims.limitations: Benefits require extended supplementation (weeks), GI side effects are common, and effect sizes vary by population and extract.
claims.best_fit: Long-term sustained-attention optimization in cognitively healthy adults willing to tolerate an 8-12 week onset window.
claims.not_best_fit: Acute focus demands, GI-sensitive individuals, or situations requiring immediate effect.

claims.claim_statement: Bacopa may reduce working memory load and improve cognitive performance under conditions of mental complexity.
claims.evidence_summary: RCTs and meta-analyses report improvements on working memory and spatial memory tasks, suggesting a capacity benefit relevant to cognitive load reduction.
claims.limitations: Effects are modest and slow-onset; evidence for extreme cognitive-load conditions is limited.
claims.best_fit: Cognitively demanding work requiring accurate working memory over extended periods.
claims.not_best_fit: Short-term acute cognitive demands or situations where GI side effects are not tolerable.

claims.claim_statement: Lion's mane mushroom has early-stage human evidence suggesting benefits for cognitive function that may be relevant to cognitive load capacity.
claims.evidence_summary: A small randomized trial in older adults with mild cognitive concerns reported improved cognitive scores after 16 weeks; NGF-stimulating mechanisms are supported in animal models.
claims.limitations: Human evidence is limited to one small RCT in a specific population; relevance to healthy-adult cognitive load is extrapolated.
claims.best_fit: An exploratory add-on for users interested in neurotrophin pathways who can accept a weak evidence base.
claims.not_best_fit: Acute needs, strong-evidence requirements, or use as a primary cognitive support without other strategies.

claims.claim_statement: Ginkgo biloba has moderate human evidence for supporting attention speed and accuracy, particularly in populations with attention-related concerns.
claims.evidence_summary: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses confirm attention and processing speed benefits across multiple trials, with the clearest signals in older adults or those with cognitive complaints.
claims.limitations: Effects in young healthy adults are less consistent; anticoagulant interaction context is a routine safety check.
claims.best_fit: Attention and processing speed support in adults with concern about age-related cognitive changes.
claims.not_best_fit: Anticoagulant users, pre-surgical contexts, or when seeking primarily neuroprotective rather than acute attention effects.

claims.claim_statement: Rhodiola rosea has the clearest evidence base among adaptogens for reducing mental fatigue and cognitive performance decline under sustained demands.
claims.evidence_summary: RCTs in physicians and students under high-load conditions report reduced mental fatigue, improved cognitive endurance, and subjective well-being. Effect onset is within days to 2 weeks.
claims.limitations: Evidence is moderate and extrapolation to everyday decision fatigue requires care; stimulant-like effects may disrupt sleep if used late in the day.
claims.best_fit: Decision fatigue and cognitive burnout in high-workload contexts, especially when stress overlap is present.
claims.not_best_fit: Bipolar-spectrum risk without clinician oversight, or late-day use in sleep-sensitive individuals.

safetyNotes.warning: Bacopa monnieri commonly causes GI side effects including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramping, particularly at higher doses or when taken without food.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Take Bacopa with food and reduce dose if GI symptoms emerge.

safetyNotes.warning: Bacopa has demonstrated uterotonic activity in animal models and is considered unsafe to use during pregnancy.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Do not use Bacopa as a self-directed focus supplement during pregnancy.

safetyNotes.warning: Ginkgo biloba has antiplatelet activity and may potentiate anticoagulant medications (warfarin, aspirin, NSAIDs), increasing bleeding risk.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Review anticoagulant or blood-thinning medication context before use; avoid self-directed use in pre-surgical settings.

safetyNotes.warning: A contaminant (ginkgotoxin) present in poorly processed Ginkgo can cause seizures; product quality and standardization matter for safety.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Use only standardized 24% flavone glycosides / 6% terpene lactone extracts; avoid crude or unstandardized preparations.

safetyNotes.warning: Stacking L-theanine with alcohol, sedatives, or multiple calming agents may produce unpredictable sedation levels.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Avoid stacking until single-ingredient response and medication context are established.

safetyNotes.warning: Lion's mane is a mushroom allergen; individuals with mushroom or fungal allergies should approach with caution.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Start with a low dose and monitor for itching, rash, or respiratory symptoms before regular use.

safetyNotes.warning: Rhodiola has mild stimulant-like effects and can cause insomnia, irritability, or overstimulation when combined with other stimulant compounds or taken late in the day.
safetyNotes.decision_effect: Avoid late-day dosing; reduce dose or avoid stacking with other stimulants if jitteriness or sleep disruption emerges.
